rag_rules: When AGREE diagnostics report unresolved references or type errors, trace the root cause to visible identifiers in the current component scope and avoid introducing new architecture.
Fix type mismatches by ensuring expressions match the expected scalar types (int, real, bool) and that all identifiers are properly declared or visible.
If diagnostics point to implementation-level features in a component type annex, move valid bindings to the implementation scope or remove invalid clauses.
Do not add new package-qualified identifiers or data types that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: AGREE expressions must maintain type consistency; numeric, Boolean, and package-qualified references must align with declared types and available model units.
Only use visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes; avoid referencing subcomponent paths or implementation-only elements in type-level contracts.
When fixing AGREE diagnostics, prioritize removing or rewriting clauses that reference invalid identifiers rather than adding new architecture.
Ensure that all AGREE scalar type declarations use correct names (int, real, bool) and that expressions match the expected types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and should be placed in appropriate scopes (type or implementation).
Assignments in implementation scope must reference valid output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Guarantees in component type annexes should relate visible inputs and outputs, not subcomponent paths.
Helper functions and constants should only be used when defined in the same annex or imported from a configured package.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new package-qualified identifiers or data types that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes; move them to implementation scope instead.
Do not attempt to resolve missing identifiers by inventing new declarations; instead, remove or rewrite clauses using only visible identifiers.
Do not mix scalar types inconsistently in expressions; ensure all operands and results match expected types (int, real, bool).
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
